rootCause: `frontend/scripts/setup-test-env.js` ensured Astro build artifacts but did not refresh `frontend/src/generated/build_meta.json`. In fresh environments this file remained as the checked-in static placeholder (`gitSha: "missing", source: "static"), causing `/build-meta.json` to return HTTP 503 during Playwright runs and surfacing as a nav smoke console error on `/chat`.
resolution: Updated `frontend/scripts/setup-test-env.js` to run `scripts/write-build-meta.mjs` before the Astro build check, with placeholder detection and a guarded fallback to existing valid metadata. This guarantees non-placeholder build metadata for Playwright E2E runs in fresh/container environments.
